intro: This technical reference explains SaaS Idea Manager: what matters now, what is fragile, and how the pieces fit together. For a guided walkthrough, start with the Project Guide.

sections.title: Read This First
sections.summary: Start here. What this project is, what it deliberately is not, and how to use this edition without getting lost in the source tree.

sections.title: Current Risks
sections.summary: The failure modes that matter today: architectural drift, schema mismatch, and the hidden cost of adding multi-user features later.

sections.title: What Changed
sections.summary: Recent editorial updates: clarified migration practice, documented the no-auth boundary, and expanded the project map.

sections.title: How the Project Works
sections.summary: A concise mental walkthrough of request flow, SQLite storage, and how ideas move from form input to persisted records.

sections.title: Important Decisions
sections.summary: Why SQLite, why no authentication, why manual migrations, and which of those choices should not be reversed casually.

sections.title: Where Bugs Usually Start
sections.summary: The places defects tend to appear first: migration drift, template assumptions, and empty-state handling in the ideas list.

sections.title: Project Map
sections.summary: A practical map of directories and responsibilities so you can find the right file before you ask an agent to change anything.

sections.title: Editorial Status
sections.summary: What this edition covers well, what remains thin, and what should be updated the next time the application changes.
